title: Reapply "[AMDGPU] Add InstCombine rule for ballot.i64 intrinsic in wave32 mode."
summary:
The developers generalized the ballot intrinsic optimization by identifying the core pattern: a ballot call with a constant boolean argument can be folded to a known result. They extended this from a single test case (`ballot_nocombine_64`) to two canonical cases (`ballot_one_64` and `ballot_zero_64`), covering both true and false constants.

The key insight was recognizing that ballot with `i1 true` returns a full mask (~0), while `i1 false` returns zero. This transforms a runtime intrinsic call into a compile-time constant, eliminating the instruction entirely. The patch implements this by checking if the argument is a constant boolean, then replacing the ballot call with the appropriate constant.

For developers: when generalizing optimizations, first identify the specific pattern, then enumerate all constant input possibilities. Create minimal test cases for each constant variant to validate the transformation. This approach turns a narrow optimization into a robust, reusable rule applicable to any constant argument.
